Name: Mrs shameem s
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am in favour of this proposal as it will create a safe and supportive environment for all
community members and specially young teenagers. Having a dedicated space where they can
gather, learn new skills, and interact with positive role models of older residents is incredibly
important. This development will help guide our youth towards healthier choices while also giving
older residents opportunities to engage, volunteer, and share their experience. It is an investment
in the longterm wellbeing of our community.

Name: Mr Erman Orhan
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application

Name: Mr Talal Osmani
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
Dear Ms Davidson,
I am writing to express my support for Planning Application DM2025/01591 concerning the
Ambulance Station on Dorset Road, Belmont SM2 6JH.
I have lived locally for more than 12 years and am well acquainted with the nature and operation of
the surrounding area. Having considered the submitted proposals, I am supportive of the change
of use from Sui Generis (Ambulance Station) to a mixed-use Class F1 (Place of Worship) and
Class F2 (Community Hub), along with the associated extensions.
The scheme would provide clear community benefits aligned with planning policy objectives. The
introduction of a community hub offering health and wellbeing initiatives, inclusive facilities,
educational programmes for children, and dedicated spaces and activities for women would make
a meaningful contribution to local social infrastructure and strengthen community cohesion.
In terms of built form, the proposed part one, part two-storey extensions and modest height
increase appear appropriate and proportionate. The overall scale and design reflect the character
of the surrounding residential area and do not amount to overdevelopment of the site.
In my view, the proposed use would operate at a relatively low intensity when compared with other
forms of development that could lawfully be pursued on the site. The inclusion of 12 on-site
parking spaces, including 2 disabled bays, together with cycle storage, indicates that transport and
accessibility matters have been carefully considered. The retention of existing greenery also
ensures that the site will remain visually compatible with its surroundings.
It is further acknowledged that Friday prayers are intended to be staggered to avoid overlap with
nearby school pick-up times, demonstrating a considerate and proactive approach to managing
traffic. On this basis, I do not believe the proposal would result in significant or unacceptable
impacts in terms of traffic, noise, or lighting.
For these reasons, I kindly request that the Council approves Planning Application
DM2025/01591.
Yours sincerely,
Talal Osmani

Name: Mrs Gloria Claydon
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I object due to the volume of traffic and parking that will become unbearable for all
residents. With two blind corners, two bus routes, school traffic, and total lack of parking spaces
it's an accident and a liability waiting to happen.

Name: Mr Riyez s
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I support this planning application because the proposed faith and community space will
bring people together in a positive and inclusive way. It will offer a welcoming environment for
residents of all ages, backgrounds, and beliefs to gather, socialise, and build stronger
relationships. Creating a place where people can meet regularly will help strengthen community
harmony, reduce isolation, and encourage mutual understanding. Its central and accessible
location makes it easy for everyone to attend, ensuring the whole community can benefit.

Name: Mr Talha Osmani
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
Dear Ms Davidson,
I write in support of Planning Application DM2025/01591 in respect of the Ambulance Station,
Dorset Road, Belmont SM2 6JH.
I have been a local resident for approximately 12 years and I am familiar with the character and
functioning of the surrounding area. Having reviewed the submitted proposals, I support the
change of use from Sui Generis (Ambulance Station) to a mixed-use Class F1 (Place of Worship)
and Class F2 (Community Hub), together with the associated extensions.
The proposal would deliver clear and policy-aligned community benefits. The provision of a
community hub enabling health and wellbeing programmes, inclusive facilities, educational
activities for children, and a positive contribution to local social infrastructure and community
cohesion.
The use proposed is, in my view, relatively low intensity compared to alternative forms of
development that could lawfully come forward on this site. The provision of 12 on-site parking
spaces, including 2 disabled bays, together with cycle racks, demonstrates that transport and
accessibility considerations have been properly addressed. The retention of greenery further
ensures the site remains visually appropriate within its setting.
It is also noted that Friday prayers will be staggered to avoid conflict with local school pick-up
times, reflecting a proactive and responsible approach to traffic management.
On this basis, I do not consider that the proposal would give rise to significant or unacceptable
traffic, noise, or light impacts.
For all of the the above reasons, I sincerely request that the Council grants approval for Planning
Application DM2025/01591.
Yours sincerely,
Talha Osmani

Name: Mrs Deepa Patel
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
The increased level of cars on this very dangerous cross way will impact school
children's safety heavily.
The increased traffic on a heavily built up area of traffic will also impact largely on households
neighbouring the centre.
Along with all of the above it will be more noise and directly backs onto our residential property
which impacts our privacy with direct view from our and our children's bedroom windows.

Address: Tylers End 74 Grange Road Sutton SM2 6SN
Name: Mr Peter Davies
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
We don't need any more places of worship, community hubs or similar in this area. This
is a residential area and the increase in traffic, parking issues and noise that would inevitably
result due to the increase in people wanting to attend such a facility is not desirable in a residential
area and would be detrimental to those that live in the area.

Name: Mr Zuhaib TAIMURI
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I have been a local resident in Sutton for the best part of 20 years. Growing up there
was a lack of youth activities and I felt there was not that much of a community. I got introduced to
BASMA about 5 years ago and have been attending regular congregations since. BASMA brings
about a sense of community in the locality and over the last 20 years this was something that was
lacking. They regularly host discussions, festivals, get togethers, careers talks, and do activities
which support the youth and the most vulnerable in our society. The community came together to
fund the purchase of the Ambulance centre and this is largely a community led project by local
residents, for the local residents. This project is to create a central location where BASMA can
base themselves and continue to provide benefit to the local community. Whilst BASMA has put in
permission for a place of worship, it keeps its doors open to people of all faiths and also to those
that do not follow a particular faith. Should the permission be granted, the developed
accomodation can host youth clubs, recreational halls and community get togethers.
With respect to the property itself, this has been a derelict ambulance centre for many years. It
sold at auction a few years.The application submitted by BASMA makes good use of this land
which would have otherwise been wasted. The NPPF and current Government agenda is to
promote the development of brownfield sites and this application for the ambulance centre fits this
category. The proposed plans makes good majority of the existing structure and building footprint
suggesting the environmental impact of the development will be minimal as opposed to
keeping a derelict site in the locality. A small extension has been proposed, however this is
towards the rear of the building and has been incorporated in the design to maintain a female
friendly use of the space encouraging diversity and inclusion. The planning application has taken
into consideration parking provisions by incorporating 12 onsite parking provisions and 2 disabled
parking spaces. The operational times have factored in school pick up timings thereby not causing
unnecessary strain on local infrastructure. The biodiversity of the site has been factored in
maintaining green spaces, and a cycle storage facility is also incorporated within the design. The
location is such where local residents can walk to the site. This suggests that the design and
location has great consideration for the environment and general wellbeing of individuals.
One similar development in the area which comes to mind is the Church at the bottom end of
Northey Avenue. Regular congregations occur here and on street and off street parking is
incorporated however this use of their site is welcomed by locals and has not had a severe impact
on local traffic. This Church does bring together community and I am very confident that should
the application for the ambulance centre be granted, a similar positive effect would take place in
the area.
In conclusion, I feel the application for the ambulance centre is a proposed development which I
feel will greatly benefit the local community and help promote Sutton, Belmont, and Cheam as a
welcoming, diverse, and considerate neighbourhood. I therefore humbly request that this
application is given the positive light that it deserves and that it is granted over an overwhelming
support from the locals in the area.
Regards,
Mirza Zuhaib Bakht Taimuri

Name: Mr Muhammad Bin-Salman
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
A convenient, local space to network and engage with the community and provide vital
services across all age groups that would also help with positive mindset and mental health.

Address: 12 Heathfield Court 4 Cedar Gardens Sutton SM2 5EQ
Name: Mr Vineet Madan
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment: Traffic congestion

Address: 12 Heathfield Court 4 Cedar Gardens Sutton SM2 5EQ
Name: Ms Lipsa Karmiani
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment: Too close to school and trafgic

Name: Dr Zain Mirza
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application

Name: Mr Ashil Sidar
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
Given the number of single-purpose religious facilities already in the area, the council
should consider whether this site would better serve long-term community cohesion for a multi
faith attraction such as sports facilities for the youth of the area.

Name: Mr Zain Tahir
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am writing as a local resident to strongly support planning application DM2025/01591
for the proposed community centre. This facility will be a real asset to the area, providing a
welcoming space for residents of all ages. The dedicated women's and family provision is
especially important, as it will help many local people participate more fully in community life.
The application has been carefully prepared, and the supporting assessments confirm that the
centre will operate within existing noise levels and without causing parking or traffic issues. The
site is a vacant brownfield property, and bringing it back into productive community use is far
better than leaving it unused.
I respectfully ask the Council to approve this application for the benefit of the local community.

Name: Mr Sam Heard
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment: Strongly object

Name: Mrs Lorraine Fortune
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
Not 2 miles down the road in Morden is one of the biggest mosques in England. It
causes parking problems for all other residents. This is not required
This development would be better suited as a Non religious community youth centre, where
children have somewhere to go in sutton and not be hanging round on the streets

Name: Miss Sarah Hornshaw
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I'm concerning that there will be a significant increase in traffic; this is a quiet residential
area and the actual location is a cul-de-sac- parking will be a nightmare!
There is a locall school nearby and when school starts and finishes, there is a signifcant amount of
traffic and activity; the proposals for the new site will exacerbate the issues with traffic further.
I am also concerned whether arriving for prayers very early in the morning will also affect local
residents- this is a quiet residential area and it could have a significant impact on those living close
to the site.

Name: Mr Nikolay Dermendzhiev
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I object to DM2025/01591 on the grounds that it would cause unacceptable harm to
highway safety, parking stress, and residential amenity in an already constrained residential area.
I live at 34 Holland Avenue and experience regular parking congestion, particularly during school
drop-off and pick-up times. Vehicles frequently park close to junctions, reducing visibility, and
manoeuvre with difficulty due to limited road width. Pavements are often obstructed, creating
safety issues for pedestrians.
The applicant's Transport Statement identifies a worst-case scenario of up to 80 vehicles locally,
with only 14 on-site spaces, leaving up to 69 vehicles overspill onto surrounding streets. The local
road network does not have capacity to accommodate this level of additional parking without
blocked driveways, unsafe manoeuvres, and reduced highway safety.
The Fire Strategy confirms a maximum building capacity of 297 persons, and documents indicate
up to 250 attendees may depart around 11pm following larger events. Late-evening dispersal of
this scale would cause disturbance from vehicle movements, idling engines, and noise within a
quiet residential setting.
Given the constrained layout of Holland Avenue and surrounding cul-de-sacs, the proposal would
result in severe cumulative impacts on safety and amenity.
I respectfully request that Sutton Council refuse planning application DM2025/01591

Address: 4 Bernard Place Springfield Road Epsom KT17 3DX
Name: Mr Rizwan Ali
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I support this application because the change is intended to benefit everyone in the
community and not any specific group.
I believe it will help to bring people together and provide services that are particularly helpful to the
senior sector of the community.
The change in use will also improve the view from the street with the removal of the large,
industrial looking folding doors.
I also believe the intended use will not lead to any significant noise disturbance to the neighbours -
something the previous use as an ambulance centre could not have guaranteed.

Name: Mr Robert Hawkins
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I object on the following grounds:
1. The proposed extensions and increase in height would create a structure visually entirely out of
keeping with the domestic scale of neighbouring properties.
2. Increased traffic and road safety risks, in particular concerning The Avenue Primary Academy,
nearby Care Home and users of Sutton Bowls Club (which has both outdoor and indoor rinks).

Name: Mr Martin Smith
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
Sir,
This is a wholly inappropriate application based on location i.e. closeness to residential properties,
a large school with very young children attending and a local road system including bus routes
already unable to cope at school times.
There are no mitigating circumstances available in respect of the application.
The planning officer who initially gave an indication of potential permission for change of use
clearly had little knowledge of the area and made a mistake.

Name: Mr Stephen Lock
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
The objection is on the basis of increased traffic disruption and the complete lack of
parking provision for this development. With the Avenue Road school foot fall/car drop off tripling
over the last 10 years or so the disruption to our area is already way beyond what we could have
imagined. This Worship/Community hub will add yet another layer of traffic, disruption and parking
issues. We witness on a regular basis traffic jams that can last between 10 and 30 minutes in
Avenue Road during the morning and afternoon from the school drop off and pick up times. The
traffic at the crossroads at the junction of Dorset Road and Avenue Road during these periods is
dangerous with many people crossing roads on foot. I strongly object to further regular disruption
to this area.

Name: Mr Malcolm Smith
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
This change of use will create a lot of traffic and parking issues in this quiet residential
area. It is completely out of character with the neighbourhood.
As a local resident, I want to protect the character of the area. I've noticed that a lot of people
supporting the application are from outside the area and won't be affected by additional
traffic/parking.

Name: Mrs Rashmi Kanetkar
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I would like to bring to your attention the proposal to construct a two-storey prayer and
community hall at the London Ambulance Station site, close to Avenues School.
This proposed development may lead to increased traffic, additional parking pressure, noise, and
pollution in what is currently a relatively quiet residential neighbourhood. Given its close proximity
to Avenues as well as quite a few care homes for vulnerable people,there are also understandable
concerns regarding road safety, particularly during school drop-off and pick-up times.
If safety is considered a prime issue in many other matters then this issue should also be
considered as the important issue where elderly and young children's safety may get affected due
to this proposed building.
Please look into the matter before it affects the residents.
Thank you

Name: Anum Mall
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
Negative impact on nearby school and care home. Traffic around the area and pressure
on parking in nearby streets with increased traffic incident risks.

Name: Mrs Neha Chopra
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I object the building of Mosque in the area of SM2 mainly due to the below reasons:
1) Traffic, parking and road safety:
The site is immediately adjacent to a primary school. Additional visitors and onstreet parking
associated with the Community Hub would reduce kerbside availability for school dropoff/pickup
and could create dangerous congestion at peak times.
There is an increased risk to pupils and parents from additional vehicle movements on narrow
residential roads.
To add there is already a community Hub available at St. Johns Church which serves a place for
gathering and worship.
2) Impact on vulnerable residents / residential amenity:
A care home is located directly opposite the proposed site. The frequency of vehicle movements,
congregations and potential amplified public announcements could materially increase noise and
disturbance for residents, adversely affecting their wellbeing.
3) Local provision and need:
There are already multiple places of worship serving the Sutton area. The application does not
demonstrate a clear, evidenced need for an additional facility at this location nor explain why other
sites with better parking and lower residential impact were not considered.
4) Character and amenity of the neighbourhood:
The proposal would increase traffic on inner roads and change the quiet residential character of
the area. already we are facing increased parking issues due to school drop off and pick ups ,a
new Place of Worship/Community Hub would amplify the traffic thereby causing disturbance to the
residents.
For these reasons I request that the application be refused.

Name: Mr Ahmet Cengiz
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am writing to the Council to express my support for the planning application at the
former ambulance station on Dorset Road (DM2025/01591). I think this is a good proposal that
merits approval.
I want to emphasise that the community hub will serve all residents regardless of faith or
background. The programme includes educational classes, wellbeing sessions, and social events
that are open to everyone.
In addition, there is no basis for the suggestion that this facility will cause late-night disturbance.
The operating hours are restricted and the applicant has set out a programme of activities that is
weighted towards daytime use.
Additionally, a community centre with restricted hours versus a 24-hour emergency vehicle depot.
The comparison speaks for itself. The proposed use is a clear improvement in amenity terms for
the surrounding residential streets.
On this point, objectors seem to have missed that the previous use had 21 parking spaces on site.
The development reduces this number, not increases it. Additionally, the yellow lines that currently
restrict on-street parking near the entrance will be removed, giving residents more kerbside space
than they have now.
Moreover, the submitted noise report confirms that the building fabric will contain sound
effectively. This meets the Local Authority's criteria. Even at the busiest time, with amplified
speech during Friday prayers, the noise reaching neighbouring properties is predicted to be below
ambient background levels.
I have read the Transport Statement and the parking evidence is convincing. A survey during the
peak Friday prayer period found on-street parking at less than 50% capacity in the surrounding
area. The worst-case modelling still leaves 66 spaces available and stays well under the 85%
parking stress threshold.
This proposal ticks every box: it is policy-compliant, well-evidenced, and really needed. I ask the
Council to grant planning permission for DM2025/01591.

Name: Mr QAMAR KHAWAJA
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I write to you as an 85 year old resident of Holland Avenue in full support for the
planning application DM2025/01591.
I have reviewed the proposal and feel that one of the greatest benefits is that this centre is easily
accessible for myself, within walking distance of our home. As we grow older, there is a great need
for facilities that can be safely reached on foot, where there are activities and events for us. This
allows us to remain independent, active and also to be connected to our neighbourhood and
residents.
I feel very encouraged to hear of the proposed programmes and activities available for older
residents. We need places to meet others, socialise, listen to talks and wellbeing sessions, but
also to spend time outside of the home in a welcoming environment. Social isolation is a real
problem for many older people in the community and having a local space dedicated to getting the
community together, to engage, and have actitivites for all ages, would make a meaningful
difference.
In my view, this centre would be a positive and very welcome addition to the area. It would support
wellbeing, strengthen community spirit and promote unity.
I respectfully ask the Council to approve this planning application, DM2025/01591.

Address: Little Friars 32 Queens Road Belmont Sutton SM2 6BZ
Name: Mr Simon Warrener
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
Concerning the application for the mosque and community hall I have just experienced
drivers on Friday the 27th February 12:45pm parking over drives in Queens Road. I find it very
funny that now the application is in that they have parking wardens (who are only monitoring the
road by the church), where previously this wasn't a concern for the people attending the pray
room. I have taken pictures to show how congested Queens Road is, but can't attach them. The
top of Queens Road normally avoids traffic from the school, but today it is gridlocked.
How the area near the proposed mosque and community hall will handle this additional traffic I
have no idea?

Address: Flat 10 Beech Court 35 Grove Road Sutton SM1 2AG
Name: Mr Razan Thahir
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am writing to express my support for planning application DM2025/01591 for the
proposed change of use of the former ambulance centre into a community hub.
As a local resident, I believe this development would be especially beneficial for young people and
the wider community. It would provide a safe, structured indoor space for youth programmes,
educational activities, wellbeing support, and positive social engagement. Facilities like this are
vital in helping young people develop skills, confidence, and strong community values.
The proposal makes good use of an existing building and fits well with the surrounding area, while
offering long-term benefits for local residents.
For these reasons, I fully support this application and respectfully request that it be approved.

Address: Flat 4 Barton Court 53 Grove Road Sutton SM1 2AW
Name: Mrs Saleha Khan
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am a local resident and have lived in the area for decades. I wish to express my full
support for planning application DM2025/01591.
The proposed centre will be a positive and much needed addition to the local community. My
family and all live in the local area and I will benefit from being able to walk to a space that offers
inclusive and diverse activities open to all faiths and backgrounds. This will support my wellbeing
by providing opportunities to socialise locally and strengthen community connections.
I am a widowed pensioner and a community center where I can socialise and join in different
activities will be an amazing opportunity. My children and grandchildren will also gain valuable
educational and faith based learning opportunities.
I welcome the centre's intention to carry out charity work that will support both the immediate area
and the wider community.
The design of the building is modest and in keeping with the surrounding landscape. The inclusion
of on site parking (12 spaces plus 2 accessible spaces) is thoughtful and practical. Importantly, the
proposal still preserves green space, enhancing the appearance of the area.
It is considerate that the application includes staggered Friday prayer times, demonstrating
awareness of local residents and helping to minimise any potential impact.
I do not believe traffic, parking, or noise will be an issue. Most attendees live within walking
distance and are respectful and mindful of their neighbours.
For these reasons, I strongly support this application and encourage its approval. The project will
enrich the lives of those who attend and contribute positively to the wider local community.

Name: Mr nadeem Hussain
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
i have just moved into the area recently (4 years) and when i was informed about the
proposal about a community centre, me and the family are so thrilled as it would make life so
much easier to meet people, we are an aging family and it would great for me and wife and kids to
meet new people, especially as many people close to us have passed away. this would help our
mental health, in meeting new people and establishing a community for our age range. also
physically helping us.
there so many community centres closing down, its nice to hear a community centre being
opened.
i have looked at the proposals and i am delighted to see the thoughtful process of keeping to the
area norms and greenery, i would like to say i came from wimbledon and the centre proposed
programmes for Friday is a huge benefit and would not affect the local area (schools) and would
keep the area traffic free, i hope this project gets the application approved and the approval it
needs to be accepted
if you reuire any further info please let me

Name: Mr Mohamad Jan Gul Mohamad
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am a local resident and strongly support this application. I walk to the local church for
Friday prayers so it would be a welcome improvement to have a community hub dedicated to our
faith activities instead of us having to use the church for it.
Additionally, it would be great to have this new centre as it will be inviting non-Muslims for general
well-being activities too.
There are some objections about traffic increasing, but most of the community walk, and most
already attend regular prayers at the church nearby and there are no traffic issues.
The site currently looks really bad and is not in use for the benefit of the community: this
conversion will turn it into a positive source of community well-being.

Address: Flat 7 Mayfield Court, 6 Egmont Rd Sutton SM2 5JN
Name: Mrs Sarah Aziz
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am writing to support planning application DM2025/01591.
In my view this proposal will provide meaningful benefits to the local community including
improved facilities and services for local residents.
From a woman's perspective, I particularly value the inclusion of a dedicated women's area within
the proposed community centre. Having a safe, welcoming and accessible space designed to
support women's wellbeing, learning and social connection is extremely important. Facilities that
recognise and accommodate women's needs help foster confidence, participation and community
engagement.
I am especially supportive of the proposed women's fitness programmes and wellbeing sessions,
which will provide accessible opportunities for physical health, mental wellbeing and social
interaction. These types of initiatives are vital in promoting healthier lifestyles and reducing
isolation.
The inclusion of children's educational classes and a crèche facility is also a significant benefit.
Many mothers face barriers to participation due to childcare responsibilities. Having a safe space
where children can learn and be cared for while their mothers attend classes or fitness sessions
will make the centre far more inclusive and practical for families.

Name: Dr Nida Khan
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am a young single woman (Professional NHS Dentist) who prefers to walk locally
within Cheam village, Belmont and surrounding areas. I've lived in this area all my life, went to
local Primary and High Schools. Having this Community Centre in Dorset Road will be an
essential part of my local community, my faith, our religion, and social activities. It is perfect for
supporting people like myself, improving community relations, social harmony and social
integration. I often bring along my non-muslim freinds from other faiths like Christian, Jewish,
Hindu to social events and cultural and interfaith activities. I can meet other young woman, single
woman, youths and vulnerable members of our community to help them improve their education,
health, faith, well being and social development. As a young Dentist working in the NHS I can see
the urgent need for this type of community centre for young girls and woman, not only muslim but
from all faiths.
I trust Sutton Council will make the right decision and grant the centre the Planning Approval
sought. This will ensure all members of Sutton population are treated equally, fairly and valued in
our society.

Name: Mr Sheer Gulzada
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am writing as a local resident to express my support for planning application.
I welcome the fact that the previous busy operational facility on the site is now being replaced by a
modest, community-focused development. The proposed centre will be significantly quieter and
much more in keeping with the surrounding residential area.
I also appreciate that the proposal includes additional parking provision and sensible management
arrangements. This demonstrates consideration for local residents and will help minimise any
impact on surrounding roads.
In terms of design, I find the development to be in line with the aesthetics of the area. The scale
and layout appear proportionate and well thought out, ensuring that the building integrates
comfortably within the existing streetscape.
Overall, I believe this proposal represents a positive change for the neighbourhood, providing a
well-managed and community-focused facility that is sympathetic to its surroundings. I respectfully
ask the Council to approve planning application.

Name: Mrs Shahnaz Hussain
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
It is essential for me to have a faith and community centre close by and accessible. It
gives me a place to meet with similar people and is an excuse for me to get out of the house as an
older member of society. It would be something to look forward to on a regular basis.

Name: Mr Farrukh Hussain
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
It is vital for me to have accessible faith and community services close by. It gives me
an opportunity to leave the house and engage with locals in a safe and enjoyable environment. It
is something I look forward to as an older member of the community.

Name: Mr ABDUL BAARI MOHAMMED
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
Re: Planning Application DM2025/01591
Dear Sir/Madam,
I am writing as a local resident in Sutton to express my support for Planning Application
DM2025/01591. I have been living in Sutton since 2020 and at my current Sutton address since
2022. As a member of the local community, I care about ensuring that development in the area is
appropriate, beneficial, and well managed.
I support this proposal because it provides valuable community-focused facilities while remaining
modest in scale and in keeping with the character of the surrounding area. The design and size of
the building do not appear excessive and would sit comfortably within the existing streetscape.
The proposal offers clear community benefits, including health and wellbeing programmes,
inclusive facilities, and wider community use. In particular, the provision of dedicated activities and
space for women is a positive and important aspect, ensuring accessibility and inclusivity. The
inclusion of educational programmes for children will also provide structured learning opportunities
in a safe local environment, supporting families within the community.
The proposed use appears to be low intensity compared to other forms of development that could
potentially take place on the site. I note that 12 on-site parking spaces are proposed, including 2
disabled bays, which demonstrates proper consideration for accessibility and parking demand.
The provision of cycle racks also promotes sustainable travel choices. It is reassuring that existing
greenery is being maintained, helping preserve the visual character of the area.
I also understand that Friday prayers are intended to be staggered specifically to avoid conflict
with nearby school pick-up times. This shows a thoughtful and proactive approach to managing
potential traffic concerns. Overall, I do not believe the proposal will result in significant traffic, light,
or noise impacts on neighbouring properties.
For these reasons, I respectfully ask the Council to approve Planning Application DM2025/01591.
Yours faithfully,
Abdul Baari

Name: Mr Sohrab Khan
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am writing to express my strong support for our local mosque and the positive role it
plays within our community.
The mosque is not only a place of worship, but also a vital community hub that provides
educational programs, youth engagement activities, charitable initiatives, and opportunities for
interfaith dialogue. It creates a safe and welcoming space for families, promotes social cohesion
and encourages values such as respect, service and community responsibility.
In addition, the mosque contributes to the local economy, supports vulnerable residents through
outreach and charity work, and helps foster understanding and cultural diversity within our area.
Facilities like this strengthen the fabric of our community and reflect the inclusive values that our
council strives to uphold.

Name: Mrs Christine Eales
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
The parking issue is impossible to solve in a general way but when parents are picking
up their children impossible. This is not a suitable site for the number of people expecting to use
this building. Residents who want to arrive or leave their property around times of arrival and
departure would find it almost impossible to do. A site must be found with a huge number of
parking spaces within the grounds of the building and with nearby roads with lots of parking space
which is not the case for the roads near this building.

Name: Mr David Goldberg
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I am a member of Sutton Bowling Club backing on to the ambulance station site. I
object to the planning proposal on several grounds whilst being empathetic to the desire to create
the faith based entre for BASMA.
1. The site is completely unsuitable for the proposed use and number of people using it. It is
actually quite a small site with only 10 on site car spaces. The plans totally fail to address this
issue apart from assuming people will park on road and minimising the impact. As I live in Queens
Road where the BASMA use the church for Friday services, I can confirm that on a Friday
lunchtime the parking is chaotic, dangerous and illegal in many instances. Visitors park across
driveways with little care for blocking drive access or making it dangerous to exit. When
considering the ambulance station site - it is even worse as the roads by the site are already used
by parents collecting from school. The parking in Balmoral Way, Bowling Way and the adjacent
flats will make the junctions very dangerous and line of sight when exiting will be so severely
restricted by many cars causing congestion. Having lived with the experience in Queens Road it is
foolhardy to think it will be any different. With virtually no parking facilities the visitors will have no
choice to park where they can. Because service participation is time sensitive, this increases the
pressure on drivers to "dump" their cars where they can to get to the service on time.
2. Impact on Belmont Community. I notice that many comments in support of the plans stress the
importance of the development to their community. However, because of the stress caused to
residents and other road users it will be the cause of friction within the wider community. The plans
are not thought through with regard to the impact on local residents and will become divisive. The
site is in the wrong place for such extensive use by so many people causing much inconvenience
to the Belmont wider community.
3. As a member of Sutton Bowling Club we have a car park for members, many of whom are
elderly and infirm. On a Friday we are particularly busy as we have several scheduled games and
social events. Because of my experience in Queens Road, I fully expect visitors to the BASMA site
to abuse our parking facility by parking - thereby making it difficult or impossible for our members
to park safely. This will therefor mean that as a club we will have to incur extra cost in preventing
unauthorised parking, such as patrols or barriers. I believe it is unreasonable for the BASMA plans
to increase our financial burden because of their poor selection of site being unsuitable for their
needs.

Name: Mrs Arlene Dearlove
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment: Object

Name: Mr Cumaravelu Balakumar
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
Re: Objection to Planning Application for Change of Use - Former Ambulance Station to
Community Space and Prayer Facility
I am writing to register my objection to the proposed change of use of the former ambulance
station to a community facility incorporating a prayer area. My concerns relate to material planning
considerations, including parking provision, highway safety, the site's proximity to a local school,
and the impact on residential amenity.
1. Parking Capacity and Existing Pressure
The site is located in an area where onstreet parking is already heavily constrained. The proposal
does not demonstrate that sufficient offstreet parking will be provided to meet the likely demand
generated by a community venue and prayer facility.
The surrounding streets regularly experience congestion, and I have personally witnessed
irresponsible and obstructive parking during busy periods.
A development of this nature is likely to attract visitors at peak times, increasing pressure on an
already saturated parking environment.
The suggestion that visitors may rely on bicycles or alternative transport modes is not practical
given the nature of attendance patterns, group visits, and the demographic likely to use the facility.
2. Proximity to the School and Conflict with School Timings
The site is situated very close to a local school, creating a significant conflict with existing traffic
flows.
School pickup and dropoff times already generate substantial congestion, with parents parking
along surrounding roads and manoeuvring in tight spaces.
Additional vehicle movements associated with the proposed use-particularly around late
afternoons-would intensify this congestion and increase risks to child safety.
The cumulative impact of school traffic and visitors to the proposed facility has not been
adequately assessed.
3. Highway Safety Concerns
The combination of increased traffic, limited visibility in surrounding streets, and high pedestrian
activity (including children) raises serious highway safety concerns.
The proposal does not provide evidence that the local road network can safely accommodate the
additional vehicle movements.
Increased instances of illegal or unsafe parking-already observed in the area-would likely worsen.
4. Noise and Disturbance
A community and prayer facility has the potential to generate significant noise, particularly during
peak attendance times or evening events.
The proposal does not include a robust noise impact assessment.
The surrounding area is predominantly residential, and increased noise levels would negatively
affect local amenity.
5. Incompatibility with Surrounding Land Use
The former ambulance station operated with predictable, controlled vehicle movements. The
proposed use represents a substantial intensification of activity.
The scale and nature of the proposed use are not compatible with the quiet residential character of
the area.
The application does not demonstrate that the development would avoid adverse impacts on
neighbouring properties.

Name: Mr Faizaan Ahmed
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
Dear Sir/Madam,
As a local resident, I wish to express my strong support for Planning Application DM2025/01591.
This proposal represents a meaningful response to a real and growing community need within our
part of Sutton.
What stands out most about this application is its strong local focus. Many of the people who will
benefit from this project-myself included-live just a short walk away. By establishing a truly
walkable community and spiritual centre, the proposal not only fosters neighbourhood connection
but also supports the Council's climate and transport objectives by reducing reliance on cars.
I am particularly encouraged by the plans for a Youth Community Group at this site. It offers young
residents a muchneeded safe and welcoming environment for:
- Career and Skills Development: Hosting local career events, apprenticeships, and mentoring
sessions that open doors for young people.
- Wellbeing and Social Connection: Creating a supportive space for community belonging and
mental health, especially for young women and underrepresented groups.
- Interfaith Collaboration: Making the facility available to multiple faiths ensures it serves as a
unifying shared resource-bringing people together rather than separating them.
From a planning and design perspective, the proposal is both considerate and well thought out:
- Traffic and Parking: The modest parking provision is entirely appropriate because most users will
walk. The planned staggering of Friday prayer times demonstrates careful coordination with local
patterns, such as school pickups.
- Scale and Design Harmony: The building's size and design respect the surrounding area,
preserving greenery and complementing the existing residential character.
Overall, this is a lowimpact, highbenefit project that strengthens community ties while promoting
sustainability and inclusion. I wholeheartedly support its approval and believe it will serve Sutton
residents for generations to come.
Yours faithfully,
Faizaan Ahmed

Name: Mr Stephen Hilsley
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
This proposal poses a serious health & safety risk to all. Avenue Primary school is a
stones throw from the proposed site, the extra road traffic and parking will create a huge risk to all
pupils at drop off and pick up times. The additional parking will reduce visibility and create unsafe
walking areas for children and parents, a disaster waiting to happen.
Where will all the addition vehicles visiting the site park, the area is already heavily
congested/saturated with parking, this will only add to the misery of the local residents.
Has anyone really thought about the noise pollution should this go ahead? Cars trying to park,
cars circling looking for a parking place that does not exist, doors opening and closing and voices
late at night?
The proposed site will be completely out of character/keeping with its domestic scale and of
neighbouring properties.
A nearby care home, elderly neighbours and the vulnerable will struggle and be hit the hardest
with the increased noise, late night disturbance and the obstruction.
The proposal would bring gatherings and events in an already constrained residential road which
is absolute madness and should not proceed.

Name: Mrs Faisa Sugulle
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application

Name: Mrs Daniya Sharief
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
Dear Planning Officer,
Re: Planning Application DM2025/01591
I have lived in Sutton since 2022. Sutton is a safe and welcoming place to live. I trust the London
Borough of Sutton to make fair decisions for the community.
This proposal will create a Place of Worship and Community Hub. It will provide educational
programmes for children. It will support young people's learning and development. It will offer
activities and dedicated space for women. These are positive benefits for local families.
The building is modest in scale. The extensions are proportionate. It remains in keeping with the
area. This is a low-intensity use compared to other possible developments.
I do not believe this proposal will cause significant traffic, light, or noise issues. I respectfully ask
the Council to approve this application.
Yours faithfully,
Daniya Sharief

Name: Mr Abul Uddin
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I have lived in Cheam with my family since the early eighties, during my time I have
observed Cheam and Sutton to have become increasingly diverse. The current congregation use
the local church in Belmont for their prayers and this is usually not large enough on certain days.
Having become much frailer in my latter years I find going there, however, a great source of
comfort and helps me to keep physical contact with the outside world. I would expect the new
centre/mosque to be outward looking to the wider community as so many mosques are and to
provide programmes for all denominations for example food banks, support for the elderly etc.

Name: Mr Fayaz s
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am writing in strong support of the proposed community center. Our neighborhood
needs a shared space that nurtures our youth, supports our elders, and brings our community
together.
Young people need safe, positive environments where they can learn, grow, and build leadership
skills. Seniors need accessible spaces where they can stay active, connected, and valued. This
center would meet both of these needs while encouraging meaningful interaction across
generations.
By hosting educational programs, cultural events, wellness activities, and community dialogues,
the center will promote mutual respect and understanding among residents from different
backgrounds. In a time when division is common, spaces that build unity are more important than
ever.
Approving this project will strengthen the social fabric of our community and create lasting benefits
for generations to come.

Name: Mrs maria Burnell
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I am objecting to this change of use for various reasons as I do not feel there is
sufficient information given to support the change.
The main reason is There is no parking provided in the plans. It is not workable in this area to run
a community centre without parking, If this is to be a place of worship that would involve afternoon
prayers with the proximity to the school this area is already really over crowded and dangerous at
times of day because of the school traffic to then add in afternoon prayers on top will make this
more of an issue, I therefore would like to understand where this traffic will go and parking so not
to cause problems for the residents.
If this is a place of worship will there be call to prayer as this is intrusive for local residents
As a community centre will this be accessible for all faiths?

Name: Dr Afsheen Wasif
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I am writing to express my strong support for the proposed Community Centre and
Mosque as per planning application DM2025/01591.
I am a resident of Mulgrave road for 6 years, I work as Doctor in Kings College Hospital and a
mother of two young daughters. One of my daughter attended Avenue primary school.
This area lack community space, nearest one is at the Town centre. This project represents an
invaluable opportunity to offer community hub in our area and is excellent use of disused site.
The mosque and community centre together will create a warm, welcoming space for people of all
backgrounds, a place that brings neighbours together.
As a doctor, I have a deep appreciation for safe, inclusive, and supportive spaces that contribute
to the health and well being of families and the wider community.
Every day, in my work as a doctor, I meet people whose challenges could be eased, not by
medicine alone but by connection, belonging, and access to supportive communal space
especially for the elderly.
This centre will provide immense benefits to the youth, providing educational , social and safe
space outside school hours
The mosque component of the development is equally valuable. It is not only a place of worship
but also a centre for charity, community service, open days, and outreach programmes. I have
seen other mosques offering warm spaces for the residents.
Parking and noise objections are speculative, roads around the Avenue Primary School gets
congested at school start and finish time, however community centre timing can be easily
managed and will not add to the congestion,
As a doctor, I know the positive impact such facilities can have on physical and mental health. As
a mother, I see the immense value this centre will bring to my daughters and other children in the
community. And as a resident, I believe this development will strengthen our neighbourhood in
meaningful and lasting ways.
As a doctor, a mother, and a neighbour, I urge the council to approve this application.

Name: Mrs Ziyu Wei
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
We object to this application. The site is in a quiet residential area and close to a school.
A place of worship and community hub will increase traffic, parking pressure, and noise, harming
the peaceful character of the neighbourhood. There are also concerns that places of worship can
become targets for extremist or anti-religious incidents, creating potential safety risks for nearby
residents and the school. The proposal is unsuitable for this location.

Name: Mrs MINAL PATEL
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I object to this planning application due to the potential risk of excessive traffic noise
and disturbance in a residential area that already has a large school and several care homes.
I have kids and worry about cars and vehicles causing pollution noise and safety risks.
This area is residential and should not be suitable for a large mosque or community centre there is
simple not enough space or parking available to accommodate.
Additionally late night noise and events will cause disruption to the residential community living on
my road and surrounding roads.
The area of Sutton already has several mosques in the borough meaning that worshippers can
travel a short distance to those already established and existing establishments.
As this area has many pedestrians and a park with numerous children the increase in cars and
vehicles could cause safety risks and fatalities.

Name: Miss Sangeeta Bhuchar
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
Dorset Road and surrounding area is a quiet residential area and we would like to keep
it that way . Putting a large place of worship (mosque) in the middle of this quiet area would
severely affect the tranquility of the area due to the increased number of people coming to the
area and also the increased traffic.
In addition I have major concerns about the following
1. Increased traffic and road safety risks - as a local resident I already have trouble coming out of
my drive during the school hours as there are so many cars parked everywhere and reduced
visibility on the road. Adding additional traffic would only make this problem worse. There is very
limited parking on Dorset Road so all the cars will be parked on Holland Avenue / York Road /
Avenue Road causing us, the residents, difficulty driving and parking .
In addition there are many school children (from the Avenue School) on these roads so any
additional traffic may also compromise their safety .
2. Serious noise and Late night disturbance - We live in a quiet residential area and do not want
noise from people driving / going to their cars / slamming car doors / chatting all day and
especially as they leave the centre in the evenings - possibly going on past 11pm at night.
3. Care homes in the area - there are care homes on Dorset Road - the residents would be
seriously affected by the increased traffic, noise and footfall in the area. They are vulnerable
people and do deserve some peace.
4. I can see that in the concerns from other people they mention Friday prayer times as the main
concern for additional traffic / people coming here but in reality I do not believe that it will only be
restricted to Fridays - there will be continuous people coming on all days including weekends and
all major islamic festival days.

Name: Mr Muhammad Rahman
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I very much support this application, I am a local resident and live 2 roads away only.
This brings back into use a disused site, and provides a much needed community space. There
are a lot of elderly people in the community facing social isolation, this would be an important
space to reduce isolation and the burden on the NHS.
Parking is not an issue for us and most of the local potential users, as many would be walking.
There are already churches and schools nearby, so parking would not suddenly become a new
issue for a "quiet neighbourhood", it's not quiet anyway due to the school. The local church hall is
already being used for Friday prayers, without any undue impact, so there is no additional parking
needs to what is already the case at the moment.
The community space is open to the whole community to use (multipurpose hall), and should be
welcomed by all communities.

Name: Mr Yahya Amin
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I wholeheartedly support this application. The transformation of the ambulance centre
into a community centre will be a space full of life, connection, and purpose. Our community needs
a safe and welcoming space where people can come together to build friendships and support one
another.
This centre is particularly important for local Muslims who will be able to socialise in a relaxed
setting, build community bonds, and feel a true sense of belonging. It will help combat loneliness,
improve well-being, and create something positive for our community. It is a chance to invest in
people, unity, and a positive future for our community.

Name: Mrs Hema Vishnu
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
Formal Objection
I wish to formally object to the above application on the following material planning grounds:
1. Highway Safety and Parking Pressure
The proposal does not appear to provide sufficient on-site parking to accommodate the anticipated
level of use. This is highly likely to result in overspill parking into surrounding residential streets,
which are already constrained in width and capacity.
Of particular concern is the proximity of a large primary school in the immediate area, with over
1,500 pupils. The surrounding roads are already heavily congested with significant vehicle
movements, parents parking on surrounding streets, and high pedestrian activity. Many School
staff park on these nearby roads as well, keeping the parking spaces nearly occupied at all times.
So there is not much room for extra parking. Many parents attend with young children and
buggies, and some older children walk home independently.
Additional overspill parking and increased vehicle movements associated with this proposal would
further reduce visibility at junctions, restrict safe crossing points, and significantly increase risk to
children and families. The cumulative impact of school-run congestion combined with new peak-
time demand would materially worsen highway safety conditions.
2. Peak-Time Congestion and Pedestrian Safety
The proposed use is expected to generate concentrated traffic flows during peak periods,
including evenings and weekends, which may overlap with school activities and local traffic
patterns. The surrounding road network already experiences congestion and informal parking
during peak school hours.
The combination of increased traffic, reduced road capacity due to overspill parking, and high
pedestrian footfall - particularly involving children and families - creates a serious pedestrian safety
concern. The intensification of vehicle movements in an area already under pressure raises clear
risks for vulnerable road users.
Parking occurs on both sides of the road, which operates as a two-way traffic route, significantly
narrowing the carriageway. As a result, buses and other vehicles have considerable difficulty
manoeuvring between parked cars, frequently creating bottlenecks around junctions and
contributing to congestion and safety concerns.
The addition of further vehicles parking on these already constrained roads will only exacerbate
the existing congestion and increase risks to other road users, particularly vulnerable pedestrians
such as young children and elderly residents.
3. Impact on Residential Amenity
The scale and intensity of the proposed use may result in increased noise, disturbance, vehicle
movements, and activity levels extending into evenings. This would alter the quiet residential
character of the area and negatively affect neighbouring residents' living conditions.
4. Emergency Vehicle Access
Overspill parking and congestion may obstruct access for emergency vehicles. Given the
constrained nature of surrounding roads and existing peak-time congestion, there is a genuine risk
that emergency services could face delays, which is a significant public safety concern.
5. Reliance on Management Measures
The proposal appears to rely heavily on management plans and behavioural controls to mitigate
its impacts. Such measures are difficult to monitor and enforce over the long term. Planning
decisions should be based on sustainable, built-in design solutions rather than reliance on ongoing
operational management to address fundamental capacity and safety issues.
For the reasons outlined above, I believe the proposal would result in unacceptable impacts on
highway safety, pedestrian safety, residential amenity, and emergency access, and therefore
should be refused.

Address: 42 Kew Crescent North Cheam Sutton SM3 9RT
Name: Mr Mohammad Bukhari
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
The centre is needed for the Muslim Community and also for the wider Local
Community. The traffic will not be increased as we are already using Saint. John's Church nearby.
Many local people will walk and not use a car. The volume of people going to the Masjid will not go
up. The increase in use will be if they take in other Non Muslim groups at different times so that
they do not coincide with the Daily 5 prayers. This is for the Future of the Local Community, both
Muslims And Non Muslims. There is a definite need for both Communities to interact to increase
Peace and Understanding between Communities. Parking will be available inside the Ex
Ambulance Centre, hence , there will not be an excess Amount of parking on the roads. I myself
witnessed a great deal of traffic due to the School on Dorset Road. That was very chaotic. How did
the Council approve the Building of the School which causes major chaos daily at school times.?
The Council has not done anything about this daily traffic jam. !

Name: Mr Kevin Smith
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I totally object to the planning application DM2025/01591 on the grounds of public
safety and inconvenience to residents ( including late night functions)
The proposed location is in a quiet residential area which includes a care home nearby and a
primary School. The traffic situation is very bad now with the school finishing at various times of
the day, and the proposed development will only make matters worse.
To only have 12 parking bays onsite with the proposed amount of vehicles entering the area is
inadequate. Looking ahead eventually more and more people may want to use the proposed
facility leading to more vehicles due to poor public transport facilities in the area.
For the above reasons I object to this planning application.

Name: Mrs Gillian Jennison
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I am very concerned re the planning application for the old Ambulance Station in Dorset
Road
We already have to contend with the traffic from the Avenue school;the Tennis Club;and the Bowls
club all in the immediate vicinity, and the fact that this is all on a bus route makes negotiating
traffic, whether as a driver or pedestrian, at certain times of the day,positively dangerous . I can
imagine how much more hazardous this will be should this planning application be approved
and traffic and footfall increase considerably so I hope it will be refused

Name: Mrs Danielle HEBRARD
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I wish to object strongly to the above planning application. My husband and I have lived
on the nearby Balmoral Way ( a cul de sac) for nearly 33 years and we chose this particular road
because of its quiet residential location and appeal.
The local Avenue Primary School has expanded significantly over the years from 400 pupils to
over 1000 resulting in it having a wider catchment area. This unfortunately, has generated a huge
increase in traffic at the junction of Avenue Road/Holland Avenue and Dorset Road during the
school drop off' and pick up times. These include not only the start and end of the school day but
also the breakfast club/after school club and the nursery which operates on a half-day basis. On
these occasions, the junction becomes impassable and nearby residents such as ourselves are
unable to get out of our road as it is the sole exit point. It is virtually opposite the disused
Ambulance Station and adjacent to this junction. Many drivers also choose to do their 3 point turns
at this particular spot which add to the congestion.
Whilst we try our best to avoid using our car at these times, it is sometimes impossible to do so if
hospital appointments or family emergencies dictate otherwise.
What I have witnessed at peak times is truly alarming whether on foot or by car. Vehicles are
parked all the way up and down the adjoining roads even on the corners of the junction (in spite of
the double yellow lines) and across people's drives. Sometimes, the ice cream van is there too,
depending on the weather. There are so many parents, children and buggies that they are spilling
off the pavement into the roads and into the paths of oncoming vehicles.
The visibility is severely restricted, so much so, that there have been not only near misses but
actual accidents over recent years at this junction. My fear is that before long, someone is going to
be badly injured. The S2 and S4 bus routes are also badly affected. Sometimes they simply can't
navigate through and, on occasions fail to stop to allow passengers off.
Having a place of worship, irrespective of faiths, in this location is in my opinion totally illogical and
unjustified. Balmoral Way is also a narrow winding road with some spaces for cars to park but if
they are wide vehicles or are badly parked, it becomes impossible to drive through. I have on
occasions had to abandon my car temporarily as a result.
My concern is also how would an emergency vehicle get through if there were to be a further
increase in traffic. We also have a few elderly and less able residents who require carers on a a
regular basis and they would be severely impacted in such a situation.

Name: Mr Mark Cooper
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
The close proximity of the Avenue Primary Academy already creates traffic issues on
Dorset Road and the surrounding areas which the council already has to address with patrols,
adding a new community hub is not going to improve the situation. Will all the streets be restricted
with double yellows if this proceeds, impacting the teachers? Regarding Community Hub,
Community prayers, etc as outlined by the owners own statements, is this the local community
who have not requested or campaigned for this?
I have to question the efficient use of public spaces, currently the hall at St Johns has been used
successfully as the hub, why can this not continue? If this is due to religious reasons, then surely
the inverse will apply to this new hub, creating further segregation and division in the community.
As a local resident I do also have to question support/objections coming from Morden/Coulsdon?

Name: Mr Ibrahim Muhammad
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I would like to record my support for application DM2025/01591. The proposal to
convert the former ambulance station into a community hub and place of worship is exactly the
kind of development this area needs.
The wellbeing sessions, drop-ins, and educational programmes will serve the whole
neighbourhood and help build a more connected community. I welcome the fact that this proposal
goes beyond faith provision to include a genuine community hub which will benefit my mental
health as well as others.
I would also note that nHS England actively promotes social prescribing, and a community hub in
the Belmont area would support exactly that. There is strong evidence that community facilities of
this kind improve health outcomes by reducing social isolation and providing access to wellbeing
programmes.
There is simply nothing like this currently available in the Belmont area. This proposal would
change that. Residents who want to access community or faith facilities have to travel to Sutton
town centre or further.
On this point, I find it odd that some objectors are concerned about the impact of a community
facility when the previous use was a full-time ambulance station with sirens, heavy vehicles, and
overnight operations. The proposed use is quieter, less disruptive, and operates within defined
hours.
Moreover, the building works are limited in scale, the height increase is minimal, and the
application includes landscaping and external improvements that will enhance the street scene on
Dorset Road. The design is appropriate for a residential area.
I wholeheartedly support this application and ask the Council to grant planning permission. This
facility is needed and will be welcomed.

Name: Mrs Tabbssum Alam
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
I wish to formally record my support for planning application DM2025/01591. The
proposed change of use from the former ambulance station to a community hub and place of
worship will benefit the local community in various aspects as follows.
The programme of activities is designed to be inclusive and welcoming. This is a shared
community resource, not a closed facility. Educational classes, wellbeing sessions, family
activities, and interfaith events will bring residents together.
It provides the kind of community space that supports mental health, reduces isolation, and gives
people a reason to come together. From a public health perspective, this proposal is valuable.
This is particularly important for elderly residents and those from communities who may face
barriers to accessing mainstream services.
The application recognises that women, families, and elderly residents have specific needs that
are not currently being met locally. The dedicated provision in this proposal is a thoughtful and
practical response to that reality.
Compared to the former ambulance station, which ran 24 hours a day with emergency vehicles
and sirens at all hours, the proposed use is much calmer. Operating hours are restricted, there are
no large vehicles, and the activities are community-based rather than emergency-response.
Neighbours will notice the difference.
In addition, I would note that refusing this application would require the Council to justify that
decision against a strong policy presumption in favour of community facility provision. The NPPF,
London Plan, and Sutton Local Plan all support this type of use.
Furthermore, the Transport Statement has addressed this directly and the evidence is clear. I
understand why parents might worry about school-run congestion, but the timings of Friday
prayers simply do not overlap with school drop-off or pick-up as planned by the committee.
This proposal ticks every box: it is policy-compliant, well-evidenced, and really needed. I request
the Council to kindly grant planning permission for DM2025/01591.

Name: Mrs Tahira Chowdhury
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
Dear Planning Officer,
Re: Planning Application DM2025/01591
I am writing to express my full support for this application.
As a Muslim woman living locally, I feel very grateful that this proposal would allow me to pray at a
mosque within my own community. Previously this was not easily possible. Having access to a
local place of worship supports both mental and spiritual wellbeing.
The proposed centre will also provide valuable space for women and create opportunities to
connect with other members of the community. Through meeting other women locally, it
strengthens positive neighbourhood relationships and community bonds.
The educational programmes for children and wider community activities will be beneficial for local
families.
The building is modest in scale, the extensions are proportionate, and the proposal remains in
keeping with the surrounding area. It represents a low-intensity use compared to other forms of
development that could take place on this site.
I understand that 12 on-site parking spaces, including 2 disabled bays, are proposed, along with
cycle racks to support sustainable travel.
From my experience attending prayers with my husband at other mosques, worshippers park
considerately and follow parking rules. I have not observed unsafe or obstructive parking. Respect
for others and for the wider community is an important part of our faith, and worshippers are
guided to follow rules and regulations and to consider the wellbeing of neighbours. For this reason,
I do not believe the proposal would result in significant noise or traffic disruption.
In addition, Friday prayers are intended to be staggered to avoid conflict with nearby school pick-
up times, which shows thoughtful planning and consideration for the local area.
For these reasons, I respectfully ask the Council to approve Planning Application DM2025/01591.
Yours faithfully,
Tahira Hasan Chowdhury, ACCA

Address: 4 Thorn Court Grange Road Sutton SM2 6RP
Name: Mrs Arifa Khan
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application
Comment:
As a resident of the London Borough of Sutton, I would like to register my strong
support for application DM2025/01591. This is a constructive proposal for a site that has been
unoccupied for too long.
Half of all users are expected to walk. Only around one in eight drives alone. These are
remarkably sustainable travel patterns, and they mean the actual parking and traffic impact will be
much lower than the raw attendance numbers might suggest.
The mix of uses in this application is what makes it so valuable. The community hub element will
host activities for all residents, making it a shared neighbourhood resource rather than a facility for
one group. It is not a single-use building.
Additionally, the statutory Biodiversity Net Gain threshold is met, and the scheme includes external
greening and landscaping that will improve the ecological value of what is currently a largely hard-
surfaced, unoccupied site. The proposal delivers environmental improvements alongside
community benefits.
The NPPF requires planning authorities to plan positively for community infrastructure. Sutton's
Local Plan echoes this requirement. This application is supported at every level of the policy
hierarchy, and I would expect the Council to give that due weight.
Bringing it back into productive use for the benefit of the community is far better than leaving it to
deteriorate. The former ambulance station is a brownfield site that has been empty since the NHS
declared it surplus to requirements. The NPPF and London Plan both encourage the reuse of
previously developed land, and this proposal does exactly that.
This proposal ticks every box: it is policy-compliant, well-evidenced, and really needed. I urge the
Council to grant planning permission for DM2025/01591.

Name: Miss Iffat Chowdhury
Stance: Customer made comments in support of the Planning Application

Name: Mr Suresh Babu Darsi Venkata
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I would like to object to the proposed community hub being built very near to school.

The provision of cycle storage in the application further supports sustainable travel.
Moreover, as a parent, the family-friendly provision in this proposal matters to me. Having

There is simply nothing like this currently available in the Belmont area. Residents who want to

The former ambulance station operated 24 hours a day. The proposed facility has restricted hours.

Name: Mr Debert Stewart
Stance: Customer objects to the Planning Application
Comment:
I wish to formally object to the proposed change of use from Sui Generis (Ambulance
Station) to mixed use Classes F1 (Place of Worship) and F2 (Community Hub), including the
proposed extensions, increase in height, and provision of cycle and refuse stores.
While I appreciate the value of community facilities, this proposal raises several significant
concerns.
Parking and Highway Safety:
The proposed use is likely to generate substantially higher visitor numbers, particularly during
evenings and weekends. The area already suffers from limited on-street parking, and there is
insufficient evidence that the site can accommodate peak demand. Overspill parking would likely
cause congestion, obstruct driveways and junctions, and compromise access for emergency
vehicles.
Pedestrian Safety and Late-Night Activity:
The development would significantly increase pedestrian movements, including during late hours.
The surrounding residential streets are not designed for high footfall at night, raising concerns
about noise, disturbance, highway safety, and potential anti-social behaviour during dispersal after
events.
Scale and Overdevelopment:
The proposed part one, part two-storey extensions and increase in height represent a substantial
intensification of the site. The scale and massing appear disproportionate within the residential
context and may result in visual dominance, loss of light, and harm to the character of the area.
Noise and Residential Amenity:
Religious services, meetings, events, and gatherings are likely to generate ongoing noise,
particularly in the evenings and at weekends. The application lacks sufficient detail regarding
operating hours, capacity limits, and noise mitigation measures to safeguard neighbouring
amenity.
Refuse and Servicing:
Increased activity will result in greater waste generation and servicing requirements, potentially
leading to additional congestion, visual impact, and nuisance if not properly managed.
In summary, the proposal represents a material intensification of use that would adversely affect
parking, highway and pedestrian safety, residential amenity, and the character of this
predominantly residential area. I therefore respectfully request that the application be refused.
